# mac-setup

> macOS development environment bootstrap. Declarative Brewfile, mise versions, dotfiles, VSCode extensions, and Claude Code config — keeps Mac Studio + MacBook Pro in sync via plain `git push`/`pull`.

## What this is

mac-setup is the declarative-source-of-truth for the DELAX developer environment across multiple Macs (Mac Studio, MacBook Pro, MacBook Air). It bootstraps a fresh machine via `./setup.sh` and keeps tooling deltas reviewable by treating Brewfile / npm globals / mise versions / VSCode extensions as committed declaration files.

- Package manager: Homebrew (formulae + casks)
- Language runtimes: mise (`mise install`)
- npm globals: declared in `npm-global-packages.txt`
- VSCode extensions: per-machine txt lists (`vscode-extensions-mbp.txt`, `vscode-extensions-studio.txt`)
- Credentials: 1Password CLI (`op inject`, `op read`) — no plaintext secrets in repo
- Multi-machine sync: manual `git push` / `pull` only — no Syncthing / auto-sync (intentional, see DELAX feedback memos)

## Where to start

- [README](https://github.com/DELAxGithub/mac-setup/blob/main/README.md) — overview and install steps
- [CLAUDE.md](https://github.com/DELAxGithub/mac-setup/blob/main/CLAUDE.md) — directory map, commands, multi-machine policy
- [setup.sh](https://github.com/DELAxGithub/mac-setup/blob/main/setup.sh) — bootstrap entry point
- [Brewfile](https://github.com/DELAxGithub/mac-setup/blob/main/Brewfile) — formulae + casks
- [mise-config.toml](https://github.com/DELAxGithub/mac-setup/blob/main/mise-config.toml) — language versions
- [dotfiles/](https://github.com/DELAxGithub/mac-setup/tree/main/dotfiles) — shell / git / Claude Code settings
- [tasks/](https://github.com/DELAxGithub/mac-setup/tree/main/tasks) — cross-machine handover task files

## What this is NOT

- Not a generic dotfiles framework — it is DELAX-specific (commit author, Claude Code skills, project assumptions)
- Not Linux / Windows — macOS only
- Not for fully automated sync — DELAX deliberately uses manual `git push/pull` after a 2026-05-09 incident with auto-sync
- Not a secrets store — secrets stay in 1Password and are injected at runtime via `op`
